Gig Worker: Struggle day and night for a couple of dollars. The grueling physical toll builds profound Hardship, while watching the city pass by hones your Observation.
Food Service: Serve coffee and food to grumpy customers. Navigating difficult patrons boosts your Social skills, and people-watching provides excellent Observation.
Delivery Driver: Deliver packages around the city. The solitary rides allow for deep Escapism, while navigating urban life sharpens your Observation.
Warehouse Worker: Lift heavy boxes in a massive warehouse. The backbreaking labor builds immense Hardship, leaving your mind free to wander into Escapism.
Office Temp: File papers and make copies. The mind-numbing bureaucracy fuels your Escapism, while office gossip teaches you basic Social dynamics.
Personal Assistant: Manage a busy executive's schedule. Handling constant demands sharpens your Social maneuvering and keen Observation of human behavior.
Intern: Fetch coffee and run errands at a publishing house. You gain your first real Exposure to the literary world and learn the Social ropes of the industry.
Blogger: Write articles for an online publication. Crafting your own narratives builds Escapism and gives you direct Exposure to digital publishing.
Content Creator: Create engaging content for a growing audience. Interacting with fans boosts your Social presence, while tracking trends requires sharp Observation.
Copy Editor: Proofread and polish manuscripts. Immersing yourself in the works of others provides unparalleled Exposure to literary mechanics.
Staff Writer: Produce high-quality articles on tight deadlines. Investigating stories hones your Observation, while writing daily maximizes your industry Exposure.
Senior Editor: Obliterate weak prose and plot holes. You gain massive Exposure to the anatomy of bestsellers and navigate complex Social relationships with authors.
Ghostwriter: Write entire books for famous clients. Adopting different voices requires deep Escapism and provides heavy Exposure to elite publishing.
Full-Time Author: Quit your day job to focus entirely on your masterpiece. Living in your own worlds provides immense Escapism and constant literary Exposure.
C.W. Student: Study the theory of literature. The academic rigor introduces you to classic Exposure, though the starving artist lifestyle brings its own Hardship.
Junior Agent: Discover new talent and negotiate small deals. Pitching manuscripts builds your Social prowess and grants Exposure to market trends.
Senior Agent: Secure major publishing deals. Mastering high-stakes negotiations requires elite Social manipulation and keen Observation of human desires.
Publisher: Acquire and publish books that shape the cultural landscape. Running an empire demands supreme Social dominance and vast literary Exposure.
Literary Titan: Spot a bestseller from a mile away. Your legendary status grants massive Exposure, profound Observation, and the ultimate Social influence.
Head of Publishing: Administrate a global publishing empire. You sit at the pinnacle of the industry, wielding unmatched Social power and literary Exposure.
Focus: Improve your learning speed through intense concentration.
Time Management: Learn to procrastinate less and receive more job experience per day.
Frugality: Study the tricks of budgeting to lower any type of expense.
Meditation: Fill your mind with peace to tap into greater inspiration from within.
Typing Speed: Practice your typing to get words on the page faster.
Grammar & Prose: Study the rules of language to write more beautifully.
Plotting: Strengthen your storytelling ability to craft compelling narratives.
Learn to create lifelike characters through habit and observation.
Healthy Lifestyle: Increase your retirement age through diet and exercise. More time to write!
Flow State: Bend your perception of time through deep, uninterrupted work.
Longevity Secrets: Through harnessing modern science, increase your retirement age drastically.
Brand Management: Encompass yourself with the power of your author brand, allowing you to absorb any job or skill with ease.
Networking: Cultivate your industry connections, improving Fame gain between lifetimes.
Public Speaking: Learn to command a room, striking better deals and earning heavy discounts.
Personal Brand: A human mind is too feeble to grasp the market. Develop a powerful brand to absorb knowledge rapidly.
Media Tours: Grow your fame through TV appearances and interviews, drastically increasing Fame gain.
Royalty Negotiation: Through shrewd negotiation, multiply the value of contracts and advances you receive from your job.
Homeless: Sleep on the streets or in shelters. It cannot get any worse than this.
Rented Room: A small room in a shared house. Horrible conditions but at least you have a roof over your head.
Studio Apartment: A single, small room that serves as your kitchen, bedroom, and office. The radiator is noisy.
Suburban House: A small house in the suburbs. Provides decent living conditions for a fair price.
Large House: A building with a few rooms and a yard. Although quite expensive, it is a comfortable abode.
City Penthouse: Much larger than a regular house, boasting amazing city views. The building is quite spacious but comes with a hefty price tag.
Private Villa: A very rich and meticulously built structure with a pool and guest house. Extremely high expenses for a lavish lifestyle.
Mansion Estate: A grand residence with its own grounds and staff. Provides the utmost luxurious and comfortable living conditions for a ludicrous price.
Library Card: Access to a world of information, allowing you to learn a lot more quickly.
Used Laptop: A cheap computer used for writing exercises to improve your typing even faster than before.
Editor: A freelance editor who assists with your work, giving you more time to be productive.
Style Guide: A reference book for grammar and style, helping you gain experience in your creative work faster.
Ergonomic Chair: A comfortable, supportive chair that keeps your body and mind fresh, leaving you in a happier, stress-free mood.
Pro Writing Software: Powerful software with outlining and editing tools, providing a much easier time learning the craft.
Home Office: A dedicated area which provides fine stationary and equipment designed for furthering your progress.
Home Library: A personal collection of books, each containing vast amounts of information from life skills to literary theory.
Inspiration Potion: Instantly doubles your Inspiration for 10 minutes, drastically increasing your writing speed.
Acceleration Potion: Instantly doubles the game speed for 10 minutes, making days pass twice as fast.
Hardship: Gained from grueling, physically demanding jobs. Adds a multiplier to your book quality by drawing on your deep understanding of struggle.
Observation: Gained from jobs that require keen attention to detail. Enhances your book quality by adding realism and depth to your writing.
Escapism: Gained from monotonous or isolating jobs. Boosts your book quality by fueling your imaginative and fantastical worlds.
Exposure: Gained from jobs within the literary or publishing industry. Increases your book quality through a better understanding of market trends and classic literature.
Social: Gained from jobs requiring high interpersonal interaction. Improves your book quality by helping you write more believable and engaging characters.
